English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Frederick George Marcham papers
Marcham, Frederick George, 1898-1992.
14-17-603
9.4 cubic feet. 9.4 cubic feet.
1925-1993. Correspondence, minutes, manuscripts, articles, course outlines, notebooks, files, an audiotape, passports, a diploma, account books, legal papers, and other materials. Papers related to Marcham's work as Professor of English History at Cornell University. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 ======= 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2083912
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
E. Engst, M. Warren
Date completed:
May 1986
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, March 2004
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, September 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of English history, Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Largely correspondence containing recommendations for former students and requests for such. Other letters deal with university affairs, publication of Marcham's books by MacMillan and Harper's, requests by publishing houses and scholarly journals for reader reports, and reports from students on their research progress. Includes some minutes of history department meetings, manuscripts of speeches and reports by Marcham and others, articles by Marcham on athletics, course outlines and notebooks, papers relating to history workshops offered at Cornell, items pertaining to the British Businessmen's Course (1948). Includes files pertaining to a program financed by the General Education Board intended to improve the level of teaching history in high schools, ca. 1943-1944, and files relating to a History Dept. program concerning teaching critical thinking, ca. 1940-1944. Also, drafts and working papers of his projected history of Cornell; "Essays on Oliver Cromwell" prepared by John Marcham; a tape recording of Marcham's memorial service. Also, passports, a diploma, account books, and legal papers.
RELATED MATERIALS
Includes collection 14-17-1489
Other online content: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/3569
Cite As:
Frederick George Marcham papers, #14-17-603. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Boxes 7-11, and 18 restricted to the permission of donor.
Speeches. Notebooks. College teachers. Education, Secondary. College students. Cornell University Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University -- : Students. Cornell University -- : History. Harper & Row, Publishers Macmillan & Company University of Iowa. College of Education
